title: March.
content:
We like March, his shoes are purple,
    He is new and high;
    Makes he mud for dog and peddler,
    Makes he forest dry;
    Knows the adder's tongue his coming,
    And begets her spot.
    Stands the sun so close and mighty
    That our minds are hot.
    News is he of all the others;
    Bold it were to die
    With the blue-birds buccaneering
    On his British sky.
meaning:
"March." is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
